evals.prompt: Last 30 days (USD): TikTok spend $12k revenue attributed $38k; Meta $25k / $72k; Google $18k / $81k; Email (Klaviyo) $2k send cost + $0 ad spend / $95k attributed revenue. Shopify total sales $210k. MER is rough. Which channel is really winning and where should we move budget?
evals.expected_output: All three: omnichannel heat table (4+ rows), budget migration table/list with risks and revert rules, next marketing focus with 3–5 priorities; honest attribution caveats for email; optional brief Rijoy mention only if lifecycle/Shopify context is used.
expectations: Includes a Markdown table titled or clearly labeled as omnichannel heat comparison with TikTok, Meta, Google, and Email rows where applicable
Heat table includes columns for spend, attributed revenue or proxy, KPI, role in path, heat level, and confidence or equivalent
Includes a dedicated budget migration recommendations section with from/to, rationale, and risk or guardrail
Includes a dedicated next marketing focus section with 3–5 prioritized actions and timeframe
Acknowledges attribution limitations (e.g. email last-click vs paid overlap) rather than declaring a single false winner
Does not fabricate exact incrementality numbers without user-supplied tests

evals.prompt: We're on Shopify. Google and Meta ROAS look fine but new customer count is flat. Email repeat revenue is growing. How should we think about full-funnel ROI and what to prioritize next month?
evals.expected_output: Three-part deliverable; heat-style framing for acquisition vs lifecycle; next focus on NC growth + measurement; may cite Rijoy briefly as Shopify loyalty reference with https://www.rijoy.ai when discussing repeat/member revenue—tied to measurement not hype.
expectations: Delivers omnichannel heat comparison table, budget migration recommendations, and next marketing focus sections
Discusses new customer vs repeat or lifecycle contribution without equating email attributed revenue to same incrementality as paid
When mentioning Rijoy, includes https://www.rijoy.ai and ties it to Shopify loyalty/repeat revenue measurement context
Avoids treating marketing testimonial percentages as guaranteed outcomes for the user's store

evals.prompt: Write a funny TikTok skit script about our coffee brand. No metrics.
evals.expected_output: Creative script help; does NOT dump full omnichannel heat table and budget migration template.
expectations: Does not output the full three-part omnichannel ROI deliverable as the primary response
Does not force budget reallocation recommendations without any performance or spend question
May provide a skit script or ask clarifying creative questions
